metadata.description: Full prefix embedding pipeline rebuild: discovered critical embedding bug (article-level duplicates), rewrote text preprocessing (no lemmatization), re-embedded with correct chunk-level prefix embeddings, ran 3-stage UMAP grid search (437 configs), established cluster quality criteria, built validation framework. Session covers April 2-3, 2026.

summary:
**What was accomplished:** Full pipeline rebuild after discovering critical embedding bug (article-level duplicates). Rewrote text preprocessing (no lemmatization), re-chunked to 1,742 natural-text chunks from 125 articles, re-embedded with correct chunk-level prefix embeddings, completed 3-stage UMAP grid search (437 total configs across Stages 1/1b/1c). Established cluster quality framework (min 10 chunks + min 2 articles), built data validation module, created project-scoped rules and skills. All verified with 13-point quality checks and 159 passing tests.

**What is next:** Stage 2 grid search (60 configs × 30 seeds) with recommended params: nn=[20,36,42,45,53], md=[0.01,0.03,0.05], nc=[4,5,6,8], k=[15-50]. Must track both consensus AND per-seed silhouette. Config selection prioritizes cluster quality (fewest single-article clusters, best min cluster sizes, lower k) over raw metric scores. After Stage 2: Stage 3 fine k search → probe extraction → full pipeline → 4-model cluster labeling → human review → figures → manuscript update.

**Critical context:** The HANDOFF_stage2.md file has the complete handoff including recommended search space, all file paths, and the 9-step checklist for the next session. SESSION_CHANGES_20260403.md has all 13 methodological changes with manuscript update instructions.

annotation.content: CRITICAL BUG DISCOVERED: The prefix embedding script (scripts/prefix_comparison.py, line 206) used text_col_pub = 'text_phrase_norm' which is the FULL ARTICLE TEXT (129 unique values across 891 rows), NOT the chunk text. All chunks from the same article received identical embedding vectors (cosine similarity = 1.000000). This inflated ALL downstream metrics: silhouette was 0.97 (should be ~0.70), ARI was 0.88 (should be ~0.70), trustworthiness was 0.97 (should be ~0.92). The old no-prefix pipeline was NOT affected — those embeddings were pre-computed correctly. The bug was introduced when creating prefix embeddings for the first time. Human (Oliver) caught this by noticing identical exemplar texts across different chunks in the same cluster.

context.conversation_snippet: User: i am seeing a major issue in the data. when i look at the example data per cluster, i see that many of the text are the exact same. for example, the first 3 for cluster 0 are the exact same text. this is completely wrong

decision.description: Stop using lemmatized/preprocessed text for embedding. Re-chunk from raw text_og with minimal cleaning only (URLs, markdown, HTML, boilerplate). No lemmatization, no stopword removal, no phrase normalization. Transformer models like e5-large-v2 perform best on natural text per MTEB benchmark evidence.
decision.rationale: Investigation of chunk_text_clean showed heavy preprocessing artifacts: 'data'→'datum', 'AI-powered'→'aipowere', merged words like 'choirjpg'. All 3 chunk columns (norm, clean, lexical) had lemmatization. e5-large-v2 was trained on natural English and handles synonyms contextually — preprocessing destroys this.
decision.revision_note: User approved and directed: 'implement all of this but be sure to run verification and E2E testing scripts after each and every step'

decision.description: Do NOT normalize term variants (AI vs 'artificial intelligence', ML vs 'machine learning') for embedding. Keep natural text as-is.
decision.rationale: e5-large-v2 contextual embeddings already handle synonym resolution. Variant choice encodes register information (casual vs formal vs academic) which IS semantic signal. MTEB benchmark shows normalization hurts transformer performance.

decision.description: Cluster quality floor: minimum 10 chunks AND minimum 2 distinct source articles per cluster. Clusters failing either criterion are tagged 'document-specific' and reported separately, not silently dropped.
decision.rationale: A discourse topic must by definition appear across multiple independent sources. Single-article clusters are article summaries, not topics. Human proposed the 2-article requirement; AI confirmed it was defensible and implemented it. Analysis showed 25-33% of clusters at every k value were single-article clusters — this criterion catches them all.
decision.revision_note: User directed: implement this approach in a robust and comprehensive way. Also specified that the primary criteria for final config selection should be best cluster quality (fewest single-article clusters, better min cluster sizes) over raw metric scores.

context.conversation_snippet: User: would it be defensible to do a slight revision of the floor where it says that the min cluster size needs to be at least 10 but also clusters need to come from at least 2 different articles?

decision.description: Add proportionally-scaled n_neighbors values (42, 45, 53, 70, 87, 174) as Stage 1c. sqrt(1742)=42 is the UMAP-recommended starting point. Old nn=27 at 891 obs = 3% of data; equivalent at 1742 obs = nn=53.
decision.rationale: Human asked about how n_neighbors scales with corpus size. Analysis showed the entire Stage 1b grid (nn=17-36) covers only 1-2% of data. Proportional equivalents and sqrt(n) from UMAP literature point to nn=42-87 for n=1742.
decision.revision_note: Human accepted the scaled values and additionally requested nn=45 and nn=174 (10% of data) be included. Final Stage 1c set: [42, 45, 53, 70, 87, 174].

decision.description: Track both per-seed silhouette scores AND consensus silhouette scores in Stage 2, since the consensus space is where all downstream analysis happens.
decision.rationale: Per-seed silhouette measures cluster quality in individual UMAP runs. Consensus silhouette measures cluster quality in the final averaged space used for all downstream analysis. Both matter but consensus silhouette is arguably more important since that's the actual analysis space.

context.conversation_snippet: User: for stage 2, in addition to silhouette scores per seed, i also want to look at final consensus silhouette scores and keep track of that

decision.description: Final config selection criteria (human-directed): prioritize cluster quality over raw metrics. Specifically: (1) fewest single-article clusters, (2) better min cluster sizes, (3) most interpretable (lower k), (4) metrics (sil, ARI) only need to meet a decent threshold, not be maximized.
decision.rationale: The highest silhouette configs (nn=15-20, k=60-70) have 25-33% single-article clusters and min cluster sizes of 5-6. These produce impressive-looking metrics but poor-quality topics. The user explicitly stated cluster quality should be the primary selection criterion.

context.conversation_snippet: User: the main criteria i want to use to decide on final configs is going to be best cluster quality --> better min cluster sizes, the fewest single-article clusters as possible, and then the most interpretable (ie lower k)

contribution.description: Clean text chunking module (src/text_processing.py): minimal cleaning + 250-word/25-overlap chunking from raw text_og. Produced 1,742 unique chunks from 125 articles. 13-point verification suite confirmed no lemmatization artifacts, no duplicates, no encoding issues.
Clean chunk dataset: data/public_discourse_clean_chunks.csv — 1,742 chunks from 125 articles, natural English, verified unique, NFKC normalized, all artifacts cleaned.

context.conversation_snippet: User: implement all of this but be sure to run verification and E2E testing scripts after each and every step

contribution.description: Data validation module (src/data_validation.py): validate_chunks(), validate_embeddings(), validate_cluster_sizes(). Catches the embedding bug via identical-vector detection.

context.conversation_snippet: User: be sure to write up some scripts for the software in case others use this for other datasets where it would do some type of test to ensure that each piece of data is unique

contribution.description: Cluster quality module (src/cluster_quality.py): assess_cluster_quality() with min_chunks + min_articles multi-source criterion. get_best_k_with_quality() integrates metrics + cluster quality for k selection. TopicQualityReport dataclass with summary(), to_dataframe(), valid_cluster_ids.

context.conversation_snippet: User: implement this approach in a robust and comprehensive way so that it can be added to the script and utilities for this project

contribution.description: 4-stage UMAP grid search script (scripts/prefix_umap_grid_search.py): Stages 1, 1b, 1c, 2, 3 with incremental JSONL output, verification at each stage, progress monitoring. Also scripts/check_grid_progress.py for real-time monitoring.

context.conversation_snippet: User: proceed with the phase 1 and anything you can feasibly, reliably, and confidently do in parallel

contribution.description: Completed grid search results: Stage 1 (125 configs, nn=15-50), Stage 1b (240 configs, nn=17-36 refined), Stage 1c (72 configs, nn=42-174 scaled). Total 437 configs evaluated. Results in figures/prefix_grid_search/stage1_results.jsonl, stage1b_results.jsonl, stage1c_results.jsonl.
Session changes document (SESSION_CHANGES_20260403.md): comprehensive record of all 13 methodological changes with before/after, rationale, and manuscript update instructions.

context.conversation_snippet: User: perhaps it would be best to write out a detailed .md file that we could then use to ensure we incorporate all changes into the manuscript

contribution.description: Project-scoped Claude Code rules (.claude/rules/) and skills (.claude/skills/): embedding-rules.md, data-validation.md, hyperparameter-rules.md, validate-pipeline skill, cluster-quality-audit skill. All project-scoped, not global.

context.conversation_snippet: User: investigate what we should potentially include in rules and skills for this project
